Summary: Add payment method validation for wallet checkout
Description: As a customer, I want wallet checkout to reject unsupported payment methods so that I only see valid payment options before submitting an order.
AcceptanceCriteria: AC1: Given wallet checkout is open, when the customer selects an unsupported payment method, then the payment method is rejected with message PAYMENT_METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED. | AC2: Given a supported payment method is selected, when the customer submits checkout, then checkout proceeds to authorization. | AC3: Given the payment method service is unavailable, when the customer submits checkout, then checkout displays TEMPORARILY_UNAVAILABLE and does not create an authorization request.
BusinessRules: Supported payment methods are CARD, ACH, and DIGITAL_WALLET. Unsupported methods must not call authorization.
SourceLinks: Confluence: QA Standard - Test Case Template For Rovo Agents; Jira story export row QATCA-101
APIOrSchemaNotes: POST /wallet/checkout has fields customerId, paymentMethodType, amount, currency. paymentMethodType enum: CARD, ACH, DIGITAL_WALLET.
RequestedArtifacts: Feature Brief; Test Suite; Coverage Map; RTM; XRAY CSV
ExpectedAgentFocus: Generate source-backed positive, negative, and service-unavailable tests. Preserve AC-to-test traceability and CSV-ready structure.
SeededGapsOrConflicts: None. This row is intended as the clean happy path.
PrivacyNotes: Synthetic internal test data only. No PII or PHI.

Description: As a service representative, I want to see whether a policy document is ready to download so that I do not retry before generation is complete.
AcceptanceCriteria: AC1: Given the document status is READY, when the representative opens the policy documents panel, then the Download button is enabled. | AC2: Given the document status is PENDING, when the representative opens the policy documents panel, then the Download button is disabled and the status text says Generation in progress. | AC3: Given the document status is FAILED, when the representative opens the policy documents panel, then the Download button is disabled and the status text says Document generation failed.
BusinessRules: Document status values are READY, PENDING, FAILED. Download action is allowed only for READY.
SourceLinks: Confluence: Policy Document UX Notes; Jira story export row QATCA-102
DiagramNotes: Diagram shows panel states for READY and PENDING only. FAILED is described in AC but not shown in diagram.
RequestedArtifacts: Feature Brief; Test Suite; Coverage Map; RTM
ExpectedAgentFocus: Generate tests from AC and call out diagram coverage gap for FAILED state.
SeededGapsOrConflicts: Diagram missing FAILED state visual. Agent should add TBDLog item, not invent screenshot details.
PrivacyNotes: Synthetic internal test data only. No PII or PHI.

Description: As an underwriting analyst, I want renewal discount eligibility calculated from documented criteria so that eligible policies receive the correct renewal offer.
AcceptanceCriteria: AC1: Given a policy has no claims in the last 12 months and autopay is enabled, when renewal rating runs, then the policy receives a 5 percent renewal discount. | AC2: Given a policy has one or more claims in the last 12 months, when renewal rating runs, then the policy receives no renewal discount. | AC3: Given autopay is disabled, when renewal rating runs, then the policy receives no renewal discount.
BusinessRules: Business rule A says discount is 5 percent. Business rule B in attachment says discount is 7 percent for no-claim autopay policies.
SourceLinks: Confluence: Renewal Discount Draft Standard; Jira story export row QATCA-103
RequestedArtifacts: Test Suite; Coverage Map; RTM; ConflictLog
ExpectedAgentFocus: Generate tests for unambiguous eligibility logic and log the discount percentage conflict for human resolution.
SeededGapsOrConflicts: Conflict: discount is 5 percent in AC/business rule A and 7 percent in attachment. Agent must not resolve this itself.
PrivacyNotes: Synthetic internal test data only. No PII or PHI.

Description: As a claims adjuster, I want duplicate claim warnings during intake so that duplicate claims are not created accidentally.
BusinessRules: Potential duplicate claim if same policy number, loss date, and loss type match an open claim.
ExpectedAgentFocus: Refuse to invent acceptance criteria. Return source summary and missing AC TBDLog.
SeededGapsOrConflicts: Missing acceptance criteria. Agent should not generate final test cases unless it clearly marks them as draft assumptions or asks for AC.
PrivacyNotes: Synthetic internal test data only. No PII or PHI.

Description: As a customer, I want autopay enrollment to validate bank account input so that invalid payment setup is rejected before saving.
AcceptanceCriteria: AC1: Given routingNumber is not 9 digits, when the customer submits autopay enrollment, then the API returns VALIDATION_ERROR with field routingNumber. | AC2: Given accountNumber is blank, when the customer submits autopay enrollment, then the API returns VALIDATION_ERROR with field accountNumber. | AC3: Given routingNumber and accountNumber are valid, when the customer submits enrollment, then the API returns ENROLLED and stores masked account ending in the last four digits.
BusinessRules: Never store full bank account number in generated output. Mask account data in examples.
APIOrSchemaNotes: POST /billing/autopay/enroll fields: policyId, routingNumber, accountNumber, accountType. accountType enum CHECKING, SAVINGS.
RequestedArtifacts: Test Suite; XRAY CSV; Coverage Map; RTM
ExpectedAgentFocus: Generate validation tests, mask sample account data, and avoid real financial values.
SeededGapsOrConflicts: Privacy handling: generated test data must use fake/masked account numbers only.
PrivacyNotes: Synthetic internal test data only. No real financial data.

Description: As an operations user, I want to filter producer organizations by status and state so that I can find active producer organizations quickly.
AcceptanceCriteria: AC1: Given the user selects status Active, when search runs, then only active producer organizations are shown. | AC2: Given the user selects state AZ, when search runs, then only producer organizations licensed in AZ are shown. | AC3: Given the user selects status Active and state AZ, when search runs, then results satisfy both filters.
BusinessRules: Status filter supports Active and Inactive. State filter supports two-letter US state codes.
SourceLinks: Confluence: Producer Org Search Notes; Jira story export row QATCA-106
APIOrSchemaNotes: GET /producer-orgs?status=&state= returns producerOrgId, displayName, status, licensedStates.
DiagramNotes: Wireframe shows status dropdown but state field label is unclear.
RequestedArtifacts: Feature Brief; Test Suite; Coverage Map; RTM; TBDLog
ExpectedAgentFocus: Generate combination tests and flag unclear state field label from wireframe.
SeededGapsOrConflicts: Diagram ambiguity: state field label unclear. Agent should not invent exact label.
PrivacyNotes: Synthetic internal test data only. No PII or PHI.
